channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: 3D Realms Co-Founder Believes Layoffs Have Begun At Zenimax Studios
item.content:encoded:
Days after the reports began regarding other Xbox studios, one of its biggest first-party developers is also going to look drastically different. According to 3D Realms and Apogee co-founder George Broussard, layoffs have begun at Zenimax Studios.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news from Google Play or the App Store.

Broussard made the claims in a post on social media on Wednesday.

“Getting word that layoffs at Zenimax has begun,” he said. “Heard rumblings recently that the cuts may be much deeper than you expect.”

Rumors began to circulate earlier this week that Xbox was looking to let go of those employees within the studio who aren’t working on Fallout or Elder Scrolls. It’s something that Broussard said he was told as well.

As always, these are just rumors at the moment. Until something is officially announced, or Insider Gaming can confirm the details with our own sources, please take the information with a grain of salt.

Xbox hasn’t responded to requests for comment on the latest claims.

Dark days ahead for Xbox

In addition to the latest rumors surrounding Zenimax, Xbox is set to shut down Compulsion Games, Ninja Theory, and Double Fine. Management at the three studios are in negotiations to find a way to keep the studios alive as independent entities.

  • Some Good News | Insider Gaming Showcase Participant Smash ‘N Grab Nominated For Award

Another studio Insider Gaming has learned is at risk is Arkane Lyon. It’s worth noting, however, that it’s unclear if a definitive decision has been made.

What do you make of the claims that layoffs are happening at Zenimax? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about the latest major player milestone Peak surpassed as it hits its first anniversary. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Marvel’s Spider-Man 2 is Getting a Free Suit from ‘Brand New Day’
item.content:encoded:
Insomniac has announced that players will receive a free suit in Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, inspired by the upcoming movie Spider-Man: Brand New Day.

In 2024, Insomniac confirmed that they do not plan to release any additional story content for Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, unlike their previous title. However, they have kept players engaged with a variety of cosmetics and game improvements. There is a new announcement, it won’t be any story DLC, but players can enjoy a new suit introduced just in time for the upcoming movie.

Before we get into the full story, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Marvel’s Spider-Man 2’s Free Brand New Day Suit is Called ‘Fresh Start’

The developers announced the release of a new free suit for Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, inspired by Spider-Man: Brand New Day, through a post on their official X account (formerly Twitter). The new suit is called “Fresh Start” and will be available for PS5 and PC players through an in-game update scheduled to arrive on July 28, just days before the release of the upcoming movie. Tom Holland is seen wearing this suit in the movie’s latest trailer.

More Insider Gaming News: Destiny 2 Final Update’s Success Hasn’t ‘Moved the Needle’ at Bungie

Spider-Man: Brand New Day is set to release on July 31 and introduces players to a brand-new story featuring Peter Parker. Following the events of the previous film, everyone has forgotten who Spider-Man and Peter Parker are, leaving the friendly neighborhood superhero feeling isolated. To make matters worse, he seems to be losing control of his powers.

Fans will also see the arrival of characters like Bruce Banner and Frank Castle as they come to Peter’s aid in the face of a new threat. With this upcoming update, players will have the opportunity to suit up like the character in Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, allowing them to experience what it feels like to be in Peter’s shoes as he navigates this latest adventure.

What are your thoughts on Marvel’s Spider-Man 2 getting a free suit inspired by Brand New Day? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Black Ops and Black Ops 2 ports coming to PlayStation in July. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops and Black Ops 2 Ports Are Coming to PlayStation in July
item.content:encoded:
It has been announced that both Call of Duty: Black Ops and Black Ops 2 will be getting ports for the PlayStation 4 and 5 this July.

Before you continue reading, though, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the best way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Black Ops Ports on PlayStation

As of the time of writing, there’s been no official confirmation on whether the Black Ops 1 and Black Ops 2 ports will be making their way to Xbox or PC.

The news was first reported by the official Treyarch Call of Duty account, which tweeted on X, “It’s official: the original Black Ops and Black Ops 2 are being ported to PlayStation in July, courtesy of our partners at Iron Galaxy.”

RELATED – Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports—Release, Platforms, Multiplayer, File Sizes & Remaster Status

Call of Duty: Black Ops 2 was first released in November 2010, with the sub-series’ second installment releasing in 2012. Both games, which are regarded as some of the best in the franchise, had only been available on their original platform releases until now, of course, in July.

It’s worth noting that these are just ports of the original games, and won’t be remasters or remakes, and it’s currently unknown how Activision plans to handle the multiple servers and capabilities (which are quite outdated).

But as always, we’ll be sure to keep our readers updated as we start to learn more about the Black Ops ports.

Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord what you think of Black Ops 1 and 2 ports coming to PlayStation.

Read about Marathon players accusing Bungie of ‘Killing the Game’ with a Season 2 update for more news, and sign up for our free Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson
